name: Alcañiz - Minicentral de Díaz - Minicentral de las Vueltas Trail
name_original: Pintures rupestres de la Vía Verde del Valle de Zafán — circular por el Río Guadalope
description: Hike the moderate 6.8-mile Alcañiz - Minicentral de Díaz - Minicentral de las Vueltas Trail, exploring river landscapes and historic power s
region_intro: Hiking around Alcañiz offers diverse terrain, from the river valleys of the Guadalope to the unique saline lagoons of Las Saladas and the reforested landscapes around La Estanca reservoir. The region, part of the Maestrazgo, features a mix of rugged mountains, rolling hills, and forests, providing varied environments for exploration. The natural features include the meandering Guadalope River, which creates a picturesque fluvial park, and numerous natural depressions that form temporary saline lakes. This varied topography supports a range of hiking experiences across different elevations and landscapes. Best hiking trails around Alcañiz The most popular hiking route is La Estanca de Alcañiz Loop Trail, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding fields. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alcañiz - Minicentral de Díaz - Minicentral de las Vueltas Trail, a moderate 6.8 miles (11.0 km) path. This route follows the Guadalope River, showcasing its fluvial park and historical mini-centrals. Local hikers also love the Alcañiz: Hermitage of Pueyos and Parador Loop, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through historical sites and offering panoramic views, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Hiking around Alcañiz is defined by the Guadalope River, La Estanca reservoir, and the unique Las Saladas, with trails traversing hills, forests, and agricultural lands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Alcañiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 140 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcañiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Alcañiz?
A: Alcañiz offers a diverse range of landscapes, from the tranquil waters of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168790213/la-estanca-de-alcaniz-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Estanca de Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt; reservoir to the unique saline lagoons of Las Saladas. You'll also find trails winding along the picturesque Guadalope River, through rugged mountains, lush forests, and gently rolling hills dotted with orchards and olive groves, characteristic of the Maestrazgo region.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Alcañiz?
A: Yes, Alcañiz has several easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz loop from Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.5 km path. The 'Valdehueso-Camino Ciprés' route, though not listed in the guide data, is also known as an easier option through the Alcañizana orchard, showcasing traditional rural buildings.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Alcañiz area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around Alcañiz are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168790213/la-estanca-de-alcaniz-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Estanca de Alcañiz Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168774586/alcaniz-hermitage-of-pueyos-and-parador-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz: Hermitage of Pueyos and Parador Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering historical sights and panoramic views.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I discover on the trails around Alcañiz?
A: Hiking around Alcañiz offers a blend of nature and history. You can explore Civil War trenches on the 'Vuelta a las trincheras' route, or visit significant watchtowers and hermitages like Santa Barbara and the sanctuary of the Virgen de Pueyos on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168774586/alcaniz-hermitage-of-pueyos-and-parador-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz: Hermitage of Pueyos and Parador Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the Val del Charco del Agua Amarga gorge is home to UNESCO-listed prehistoric cave paintings, offering a unique cultural experience.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Guadalope River?
A: Yes, the Guadalope River is a prominent natural feature with several trails alongside it. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168703189/alcaniz-minicentral-de-diaz-minicentral-de-las-vueltas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz - Minicentral de Díaz - Minicentral de las Vueltas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, following the river through its picturesque fluvial park and past historical mini-centrals. The 'La Badina' route also follows the Guadalope, suitable for more experienced hikers.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alcañiz?
A: The hiking routes in Alcañiz are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from the serene reservoir views to the historical sites and the beauty of the Guadalope River.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique geological features to see?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the general landscapes, you can visit the unique saline lagoons known as Las Saladas, particularly the large Salada Grande. The region also features the reforested area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168790213/la-estanca-de-alcaniz-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Estanca de Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt;. For specific natural highlights, consider exploring the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5383041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Holm Oaks of La Sarda&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5818573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salty Lagoon of Chiprana&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Alcañiz?
A: There are over 19 hiking trails documented around Alcañiz, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. These include 9 easy routes and 10 moderate routes, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, routes like the 'Pasaman' Route offer a challenging 19 km circular trail with significant altitude gain. The 'La Badina' Route, following the Guadalope river, also features a substantial elevation gain of 497 meters over 19 km. Additionally, 'Las Verticales de Santa Bárbara' is a demanding 9.5 km route designed for trail training or longer excursions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcañiz?
A: Alcañiz can be enjoyed for hiking across various seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer hikes. However, the diverse terrain, including areas around the reservoir and river, can be appealing even in warmer months, especially during early mornings or late afternoons. Winter hiking is also possible, though some higher or more exposed routes might require extra preparation.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by interesting man-made structures or abandoned sites?
A: Yes, some trails offer glimpses into the region's industrial and historical past. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168703189/alcaniz-minicentral-de-diaz-minicentral-de-las-vueltas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz - Minicentral de Díaz - Minicentral de las Vueltas Trail&lt;/a&gt; passes by historical mini-centrals. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2752464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Equinox Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3560752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Railway Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3588537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Valljunquera Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;, which are points of interest along some routes.

Q: Is Alcañiz a good destination for hikers interested in exploring a town's historic center?
A: Yes, Alcañiz itself is a town rich in history. You can combine your outdoor adventures with urban exploration. There's even a specific route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17509721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Alcañiz loop from Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to explore the town's heritage while enjoying a moderate walk.
tips: The greenway was a former railway line that is now a greenway for bicycles and people connecting the most important towns. Along the way there are aqueducts and bridges that cross this sunken way towards the rest.
Close to the Río Guadalope there is this set of cave paintings that are found in the open air and that you have to be careful not to damage this area that has thousands of years behind it.
Small dam that is located on the same river and that is sometimes used as a sport fishing area. Quiet space to be there for a good while.

faqs.question: What is the typical hiking time for this trail?
faqs.answer: The Alcañiz - Minicentral de Díaz - Minicentral de las Vueltas Trail is a moderate hike that typically takes around 2 hours and 59 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, traversing ecosystems that may include pine forests, steppes, and wetlands. You'll likely experience paths alongside the Guadalope River, known for its meanders, and encounter historical industrial infrastructure related to its power stations.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can expect to see several notable features. These include the Val de Zafán Greenway Stone Bridge, the Rock Art Paintings Near the Guadalope River, and the Azud de la Badina. The trail's name also suggests passing by the Minicentral de Díaz and Minicentral de las Vueltas, which are small hydroelectric power stations along the Guadalope River.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While it offers diverse scenery, some sections might require a reasonable level of fitness. Hikers with some experience will likely find it enjoyable, but beginners should be prepared for the distance and potential varied terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the Alcañiz - Minicentral de Díaz - Minicentral de las Vueltas Trail are not explicitly detailed, but Alcañiz is a common starting point for routes in the area. It's advisable to check local information or maps for designated parking areas near the Minicentral de Díaz or Minicentral de las Vueltas, as these are key points on the route.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in the Alcañiz region are welcoming to dogs. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near agricultural land. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the trail's cleanliness.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Alcañiz area can be enjoyed for hiking across various seasons. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for exploring. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during those months. Winters are generally mild but can be cooler.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access the Alcañiz - Minicentral de Díaz - Minicentral de las Vueltas Trail. Most natural trails in the region are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other known paths. Approximately 49% of the trail follows the 'Alcañiz - Minicentral de Díaz - Minicentral de las Vueltas - Alcañiz' route. It also uses parts of the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán (24%) and the 'Via Verde del val de Zafán' (13%).

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities nearby?
faqs.answer: The town of Alcañiz, being a significant hub in the region, offers various amenities. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options there. It's a good idea to stock up on supplies before heading out on the trail, as facilities directly on the route are limited.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The trail is designed as a loop, starting and ending in Alcañiz, and passing through the Minicentral de Díaz and Minicentral de las Vueltas. This allows for a continuous journey without retracing your steps.
